description: Horizontal Window Splitting - Add side-by-side window splitting with C-x 3, vertical separators, and uniform split orientation

userStories.title: Add split orientation state and Window struct fields
userStories.description: As a developer, I need to track split orientation and add column/width fields to Window so the layout engine can arrange windows either vertically or horizontally.
acceptanceCriteria: Add a splitMode variable with values 'vertical' or 'horizontal', defaulting to 'vertical'
Add StartCol (int) and Width (int) fields to the Window struct
In vertical mode, StartCol defaults to 0 and Width defaults to screenWidth for all windows
When only one window exists, splitMode resets to 'vertical' (allowing either split type next)
recalcWindows sets StartCol=0 and Width=screenWidth for all windows in vertical mode
Existing C-x 2 vertical split behavior is unchanged
Existing C-x o, C-x 0, C-x 1 behavior is unchanged
go build succeeds
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: This is a foundational change. Add the fields and state variable but keep all existing behavior identical. The new fields are set but not yet used by rendering (that comes in later stories). Make sure recalcWindows always sets StartCol and Width even in vertical mode.

userStories.title: Implement C-x 3 horizontal split command
userStories.description: As a user, I want to press C-x 3 to split the current window side-by-side so I can view two buffers next to each other.
acceptanceCriteria: C-x 3 creates a new window to the right of the active window, showing the same buffer and scroll offset
If only one window exists, C-x 3 sets splitMode to 'horizontal' and creates the split
If splitMode is 'vertical' and there are already multiple windows, C-x 3 shows message 'Cannot split horizontally while in vertical split mode'
If splitMode is 'horizontal', C-x 3 adds another horizontal window
Similarly, C-x 2 shows message 'Cannot split vertically while in horizontal split mode' when splitMode is 'horizontal' with multiple windows
After C-x 3, recalcWindows is called to redistribute layout
Minimum window width enforced at 10 columns; reject split if it would make windows too narrow, showing a message
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Mirror the existing C-x 2 implementation in the C-x prefix handler. The key difference is setting splitMode to 'horizontal' and the mutual exclusion check. Use the same insert-after-active pattern as C-x 2.

userStories.title: Update recalcWindows for horizontal layout
userStories.description: As a developer, I need recalcWindows to distribute screen width evenly among horizontal windows, accounting for separator columns.
acceptanceCriteria: When splitMode is 'horizontal', available width = screenWidth - (n-1) separator columns, where n is window count
Each window gets baseWidth = available / n columns, with remainder distributed to first windows
Each window's StartCol is calculated as sum of previous windows' widths plus separator columns
Each horizontal window has StartRow=0 and Height=screenHeight-1 (full height minus message line)
Separator columns are 1 character wide, positioned between adjacent windows
recalcWindows accepts or has access to both screenWidth and screenHeight
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: recalcWindows currently takes (windows, screenHeight). It needs screenWidth too. Either add the parameter or use a package-level variable. The separator columns are implicit — they are the gaps between windows that will be drawn in a later story.

userStories.title: Render side-by-side window content and status lines
userStories.description: As a user, I want each side-by-side window to display its buffer content within its allocated column range so I can read both buffers independently.
acceptanceCriteria: drawWindowContent respects window's StartCol and Width: each cell is drawn at screen column StartCol + visualCol
Lines longer than the window Width are truncated at the window boundary (no overflow into adjacent windows or separator)
Tab expansion respects window width boundaries
Each horizontal window has its own status line at the bottom of its region, constrained to its column range
Active window status line uses reverse video (== markers); inactive uses dashes (-- markers)
Syntax highlighting works correctly within each window's column bounds
Empty area beyond end-of-line within a window is blank (no artifacts from adjacent windows)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: The main change is in drawWindowContent: all SetContent calls must add StartCol offset to the column. Line rendering must stop at StartCol+Width. drawWindowStatusLine must also be bounded to the window's column range. In vertical mode, StartCol=0 and Width=screenWidth, so existing behavior is preserved.

userStories.title: Draw vertical separators and position cursor correctly
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see vertical separator lines between side-by-side windows and have the cursor appear at the correct position in the active window.
acceptanceCriteria: A '│' (U+2502) character is drawn in each separator column between adjacent horizontal windows
The separator spans from row 0 to the bottom of the window area (screenHeight-2, excluding message line)
The separator uses default terminal foreground color (StyleDefault)
Hardware cursor is placed at the correct (row, col) accounting for the active window's StartCol offset
Cursor positioning works correctly for both vertical and horizontal split modes
In vertical mode, no separators are drawn (existing behavior preserved)
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: Draw separators in the redraw function after drawing all window contents. The separator column for window i is at windows[i].StartCol + windows[i].Width (the column immediately after the window). Cursor: in ShowCursor call, add activeWin.StartCol to the column offset.

userStories.title: Adapt window close and resize for horizontal splits
userStories.description: As a user, I want C-x 0, C-x 1, and terminal resize to work correctly with side-by-side windows.
acceptanceCriteria: C-x 0 closes the active horizontal window and redistributes width to remaining windows
C-x 1 closes all windows except the active one and resets splitMode to 'vertical'
When closing a window brings count to 1, splitMode resets to 'vertical'
On terminal resize, recalcWindows redistributes width evenly in horizontal mode
If terminal becomes too narrow for all horizontal windows (each < 10 columns), excess rightmost windows are closed with warning message
C-x o cycles through horizontal windows left-to-right, wrapping around
Typecheck passes
userStories.notes: C-x o already cycles by incrementing activeWindowIdx mod len(windows) — this works for both orientations since windows are stored left-to-right in the slice. C-x 0 and C-x 1 already work on the flat slice; just ensure splitMode resets when window count reaches 1.
